messageValues.message: You write production-quality newsletters content. Follow the brief exactly, match the requested tone, and return clean markdown with a title line. No preamble, no explanations.
You are a strict editor. Check the draft for factual gaps, tone mismatches and weak openings. Return the improved draft only — do not describe your changes.

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.topic }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Build the Brief · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Build the Brief

Shapes the item: writes `brief`, `tone`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Draft with AI Writer

### Draft with AI Writer

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Refine with AI Editor · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Writer Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Draft with AI Writer** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Refine with AI Editor

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Publish Draft to WordPress · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Editor Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Refine with AI Editor** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Publish Draft to WordPress

Publishes as a WordPress **draft** post, never live — a human reviews and hits Publish, keeping AI-authored content from going out unsupervised.

Ports: ①→ Sort Drafts Newest First · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 WordPress credentials

### Sort Drafts Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Publish the content calendar with Google Drive and WordPress in real time

**Pattern:** Social Scheduler · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Publish the content calendar with Google Drive and WordPress in real time. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual newsletters is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Required Fields Present? → Build the Brief → Draft with AI Writer → Refine with AI Editor → Publish Draft to WordPress → Sort Drafts Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to social scheduler without manual effort
- Anyone automating newsletters in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2; WordPress credentials.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Two LLM calls, not one, because a model grading its own output rarely catches its own mistakes — a dedicated critic pass, with a different system prompt focused on gaps and tone rather than generation, catches what a single writing pass misses. Publishing lands as a **draft**, never live, so a human stays the final gate before AI-authored content goes public.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Swap the Editor Model to a different provider than the Writer Model and compare the critique quality.
2. Change the critic's system message to focus on one specific weakness, like SEO or tone.
3. Add a third pass — a fact-check chain — between the writer and the editor.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your newsletters process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
